text:
An emotionalKatarina Johnson-Thompsonsaid she was ‘relieved’ after the Team GB athlete finally claimed her first Olympic medal with silver in the heptathlon atParis2024.

Johnson-Thompson narrowly missed out on gold to the three-time Olympic champion Nafi Thiam despite the Belgian all the way across the two-day event.

The Brit needed to beat Thiam in the 800m by eight seconds and as KJT crossed the line in second the Belgian was a fair distance behind her.

However, Thiam just got over the line, with a couple of seconds to spare to keep hold of her Olympic title.

The 31-year-old, though, has won two World Championship golds and now can boast an Olympic silver on her fourth trip to the Games.

‘I can’t even describe the words but I’m just so relieved and so happy that I have an Olympic medal to add to my collection,’ she told the BBC.

‘It was the only one I didn’t have and it’s been so hard getting back to this point.

What a phenomenal effort from KJT ð«¶A sensational silver in Paris ð¥Nafi Thiam did enough to secure an historic third Olympic gold medal.#BBCOlympics#Paris2024#Olympicspic.twitter.com/vRfsac1dBC

‘In the lead-up to these Games, all I’ve been saying is that’s what I want, obviously I wanted a medal but I just wanted to have a chance at the start of the 800m and that’s what I had.

‘It was a very far-off chance – eight seconds – but that’s what this sport is, it’s about getting to this point and seeing whose got it at the end.’

Thiam has now won gold at the last threeOlympic Games, cementing the Belgian’s place as one of her country’s finest-ever athletes.

Gold medalist in 2000, Denise Lewis, said of Thiam: ‘She’s been incredible, because she’s know the pressure that she’d be under to achieve this incredibly rare feat.

‘We’ll never see another heptathlete quite like her, she is sensational.’

title: Emotional Katarina Johnson-Thompson 'relieved' as she claims first Olympic medal | Metro News
description: Team GB star Katarina Johnson-Thompson has won silver in the heptathlon at Paris 2024, narrowly missing out on gold to the three-time Olympic champion Nafi Thiam.
